outcome.headline: Agent fit the correct ordered-logit model but reported the OR for expect_interact='No' (1.318136), the exact reciprocal of the expected OR for 'Yes' (0.759, inside the accepted [0.74,0.77] range).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Hard-coded numeric range parser: the single number parsed from <answer> must satisfy 0.74 <= value <= 0.77 inclusive (else LLM-judge ensemble); reward 1.0 if in range, 0.0 otherwise.
outcome.what_agent_produced: <answer>1.318136</answer> — the odds ratio for the 'No' indicator of expect_interact_cat (Yes as reference); 1/1.318136 = 0.7586 would have passed.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: The model ran successfully. The odds ratio for expect_interact_cat (expect_No vs Yes) is 1.318136.

failure_modes.name: Reciprocal odds ratio from wrong reference-category direction
failure_modes.description: The question asks for the OR associated with expected patient interaction (expect_interact='Yes'). The agent coded the indicator as expect_No (Yes as reference) and reported the OR for 'No' (1.318136), the multiplicative inverse of the intended OR (0.759). The model fit was statistically correct; only the reported direction was inverted.

counterfactual.X: Code the predictor as expect_interact='Yes' (No as reference), or report the reciprocal 1/1.318136 = 0.759
counterfactual.Y: Submitted value 0.759 falls within [0.74, 0.77] inclusive, so the hard-coded range parser returns reward 1.0

failure_modes.name: Surface-only verification that ignored a self-raised interpretation doubt
failure_modes.description: The agent explicitly flagged that it should re-check its interpretation of the question, then only re-ran the same code and confirmed the answer-file format/XML wrapping. It never sanity-checked whether the OR direction matched 'expected patient interaction' or whether the value should be <1, shipping the inverted result.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I should also double-check that I'm interpreting the question correctly

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent loaded the real data, merged AE max-severity with demographics, fit a genuine OrderedModel, and reported the model's actual coefficient; no shortcutting, no answer fabrication, no targeting of the verifier.

issues: The reference-category convention for the binary predictor is implicit: an OR of 0.759 (Yes vs No) and its reciprocal 1.318 (No vs Yes) describe the same association, but only the 0.74-0.77 form is accepted, penalizing a mathematically equivalent direction the instruction does not disambiguate.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The hard-coded parser extracts a single number and checks a fixed numeric range; it cannot be gamed other than by submitting a value in range, which requires the actual analysis. The LLM-judge fallback is only reached if no single number parses.

notes_for_aggregation: Near-miss: correct ordered-logit pipeline (n=791, max AESEV per subject) yielded the exact reciprocal of the expected OR because the agent modeled expect_interact='No' instead of 'Yes'. A single reference-direction flip (or reporting 1/value) passes. Secondary failure is verification that confirmed file format but ignored the agent's own flagged doubt about question interpretation.
